input.name: Remove ghr legacy refs
input.overview: Strip remaining `ghr` / `GHR_*` legacy mentions from tracked docs and `.gitignore`, and fix the mistaken `GHR_TOKEN` env name in the introduction. No Go changes are required (no matches in `*.go`).
input.plan:
# Remove legacy `ghr` references for `gh-wm`

## Scope (verified with `git grep`)

| Location | Change |
|----------|--------|
| [README.md](README.md) | Delete the entire **"### Migrating from the old `ghr` CLI"** subsection (lines 56–63), including the `~/.ghr` / `GHR_CONFIG` migration commands. Keep a single blank line between the preceding "Requires a recent **Go**..." paragraph and **"## Quick start"**. |
| [.gitignore](.gitignore) | Remove the standalone `ghr` entry (line 9). Root build output is already covered by `/gh-wm` on line 2. |
| [docs/content/architecture.md](docs/content/architecture.md) | In the sequence diagram (~lines 74–82), rename the mermaid participant id from `Ghr` to `GhWm` (or `WmCli`) and update all `Ghr->>` / `->>Ghr` references so the diagram no longer uses the old abbreviation. The `as gh wm_CLI` alias text can stay as-is. |
| [docs/content/introduction.md](docs/content/introduction.md) | Replace the legacy env name **`GHR_TOKEN`** with **`GITHUB_PAT`**, matching [docs/content/configuration.md](docs/content/configuration.md), [docs/content/github-pat.md](docs/content/github-pat.md), and CLI help text in [cmd/gh-wm/main.go](cmd/gh-wm/main.go). |

## Code

**No edits to `*.go` files** — `git grep ghr` and `GHR` over tracked sources finds no Go code; only docs, README, and `.gitignore`.

## Optional (out of scope unless you want it)

Untracked or local-only paths under `.cursor/plans/` may still mention "ghr" in filenames or old plans; they are not part of the published project. Skip unless you want workspace cleanup.

todos.content: Rename mermaid participant Ghr → GhWm in architecture.md

input.old_string:
Requires a recent **Go** toolchain when building from source (see `go.mod` / CI for the exact version).

### Migrating from the old `ghr` CLI

If you previously used **`ghr`** with config under **`~/.ghr`** and **`GHR_CONFIG`**, move your data and update your environment:

```bash
mv ~/.ghr ~/.gh-wm
# If you used GHR_CONFIG, set GH_WM_CONFIG to the same path instead.
```

## Quick start
input.new_string:
Requires a recent **Go** toolchain when building from source (see `go.mod` / CI for the exact version).

## Quick start

input.old_string:
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant Ghr as gh wm_CLI
  participant Host as Target_host
  User->>Ghr: gh wm_up_runner_name
  Ghr->>Ghr: Load_config_resolve_mode
  Ghr->>Host: Connect_SSH_or_local
  Ghr->>Host: Start_native_or_docker_per_instance
  Host-->>Ghr: OK_or_error
```
input.new_string:
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant GhWm as gh wm_CLI
  participant Host as Target_host
  User->>GhWm: gh wm_up_runner_name
  GhWm->>GhWm: Load_config_resolve_mode
  GhWm->>Host: Connect_SSH_or_local
  GhWm->>Host: Start_native_or_docker_per_instance
  Host-->>GhWm: OK_or_error
```

input.old_string: - **`~/.gh-wm/env`** — Contains environment variables, most importantly your GitHub PAT (`GHR_TOKEN`)
input.new_string: - **`~/.gh-wm/env`** — Contains environment variables, most importantly your GitHub PAT (`GITHUB_PAT`)

Summary of what was implemented:

### README.md
- Removed the **“Migrating from the old `ghr` CLI”** section (`~/.ghr`, `GHR_CONFIG`, and the `mv` / env notes).

### .gitignore
- Removed the `ghr` ignore line (build output is still ignored via `/gh-wm`).

### docs/content/architecture.md
- Renamed the sequence diagram participant from `Ghr` to `GhWm` and updated every message to/from that participant.

### docs/content/introduction.md
- Documented the PAT as `GITHUB_PAT` instead of `GHR_TOKEN`.

`git grep -i ghr` on tracked files returns nothing. All related todos are marked completed.
